# boards.notion.md — Notion Platform Adapter

Read `processes/boards.md` for the generic board interface this adapter implements.

---

## How Notion Structures Work

Notion doesn't have a "board" object directly. Task boards in Notion are **databases**
displayed in Board view. A database row is a "page" — that's a task.

| Notion concept | Board equivalent |
|----------------|-----------------|
| Database | Board |
| Database row (page) | Card / task |
| Status select property | List / column |
| Group by Status (board view) | Column layout |

Each database has its own property schema. The Status property name and its option values
(e.g. "Not Started", "In Progress", "Done") are defined per-database, not globally.
Inspect your database before writing queries:

```bash
bash tools/notion.sh get-task PAGE_ID | jq '.properties.Status'
```

---

## Authentication

- **Token type:** Internal Integration Secret (`ntn_...` or `secret_...`)
- **Env var:** `NOTION_TOKEN`
- **Headers required on every request:**
  ```
  Authorization: Bearer $NOTION_TOKEN
  Notion-Version: 2022-06-28
  Content-Type: application/json
  ```

See `tools/README.md` for setup instructions.

---

## Key IDs

IDs you'll need:

| What | Where to find it |
|------|-----------------|
| Database ID | Notion URL: `notion.so/.../{DATABASE_ID}?v=...` (32-char hex, ignore dashes) |
| Page (task) ID | Returned by `create-task` or `list-tasks`; also in page URL |
| User ID | Returned by `list-members` if using user assignment |

Store board/database IDs in `memory/boards/active/[board-name].md`.

---

## Script Reference

All operations via `tools/notion.sh`. See that file for full argument docs.

### List tasks in a database
```bash
bash tools/notion.sh list-tasks DATABASE_ID
```
Returns: `[{id, title, status, due, done}]`
Default filter: excludes Done tasks. Edit the filter in `notion.sh` if needed.

### Create a task
```bash
bash tools/notion.sh create-task DATABASE_ID "Task title"
bash tools/notion.sh create-task DATABASE_ID "Task title" 2026-04-15
bash tools/notion.sh create-task DATABASE_ID "Task title" 2026-04-15 USER_ID
```
Returns: `{id, title, url}`

### Update a task (change status, title, or due date)
```bash
bash tools/notion.sh update-task PAGE_ID "In Progress"
bash tools/notion.sh update-task PAGE_ID "In Progress" "New title" 2026-04-20
```
⚠️ The status value must exactly match an existing option in your database.

### Complete a task
```bash
bash tools/notion.sh complete-task PAGE_ID
```
Sets status to `"Done"`. If your database uses a different value, edit `notion.sh`.

### Archive a task (soft delete)
```bash
bash tools/notion.sh archive-task PAGE_ID
```
Sets `archived: true`. Archived pages don't appear in queries.

### Find your databases
```bash
bash tools/notion.sh list-databases
```
Returns all databases the integration has access to.

---

## Raw curl (for one-offs or custom queries)

### Query a database (list tasks)
```bash
curl -s -X POST "https://api.notion.com/v1/databases/$DATABASE_ID/query" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $NOTION_TOKEN" \
  -H "Notion-Version: 2022-06-28" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --data '{
    "filter": {
      "property": "Status",
      "select": { "does_not_equal": "Done" }
    }
  }'
```

### Create a page (task)
```bash
curl -s -X POST "https://api.notion.com/v1/pages" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $NOTION_TOKEN" \
  -H "Notion-Version: 2022-06-28" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --data '{
    "parent": { "database_id": "DATABASE_ID" },
    "properties": {
      "Name": { "title": [{ "text": { "content": "Task title" } }] },
      "Status": { "select": { "name": "Not Started" } },
      "Due Date": { "date": { "start": "2026-04-15" } }
    }
  }'
```

### Update a page (task)
```bash
curl -s -X PATCH "https://api.notion.com/v1/pages/$PAGE_ID" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $NOTION_TOKEN" \
  -H "Notion-Version: 2022-06-28" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --data '{
    "properties": {
      "Status": { "select": { "name": "In Progress" } }
    }
  }'
```

---

## Limitations

- Notion's API returns 10 items per page by default. Use `start_cursor` for pagination on large databases.
- The integration must be explicitly shared with each database — it does NOT get workspace-wide access automatically.
- Notion MCP (`https://mcp.notion.com/mcp`) is an alternative if mcporter is available — it's token-efficient and Markdown-optimized. See `processes/boards.md` for the MCP upgrade path.
